6-3.name: Computer Science and Engineering

select_groups.name: CS Track Electives
select_groups.notes: Two subjects from one Computer Science track

select_groups.name: CS/AI+D/EE Track Electives
select_groups.notes: Two subjects from any CS, AI+D, or EE track

select_groups.name: Cross-major Elective
select_groups.notes: One subject that satisfies a degree requirement in 6-3, 6-4, 6-5, or 18

6-3.notes: Out of elective subjects, at least two must be Advanced Undergraduate Subjects, and at least one must be an Independent Inquiry subject. See EECS tracks page for track groupings.

6-4.name: Artificial Intelligence and Decision Making

select_groups.name: Centers - Data-centric
select_groups.notes: Must take at least one from each of the 5 center areas (5 total). Some courses appear in multiple centers but can only count for one.

select_groups.name: Cross-department Elective
select_groups.notes: One subject that satisfies a degree requirement in Course 6 or Course 18

6-4.notes: 6.4110 can count toward model-centric or decision-centric but not both. 6.4400 can count toward model-centric or computation-centric but not both. 6.C571 can count toward decision-centric or computation-centric but not both. Must satisfy SERC requirement with at least one qualifying subject.

6-7.name: Computer Science and Molecular Biology

select_groups.name: ML Option
select_groups.notes: 6.3900 OR (6.C01 + 7.C01). If choosing the latter, 6.3900 is not required.

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: Two subjects from Biology Restricted Electives, AI+D Advanced Undergraduate Subjects, or Computational Biology lists

6-7.notes: 5.07 is also acceptable in place of 7.05. 7.093/7.094 and 7.33 can count towards either Computational Biology or Biology restricted electives, but not both.

6-9.name: Computation and Cognition
6-9.notes: Subjects from BCS Program Subjects list can count as either a BCS Program Subject or a Program Elective, but not both.

6-14.name: Computer Science, Economics, and Data Science

select_groups.name: Introductory Programming
select_groups.notes: Select 12-18 units

select_groups.name: Economics Electives
select_groups.notes: Must include at least one from Data Science group and one from Theory group

6-14.notes: 14.03 is also acceptable in place of 14.01. Economics electives must include at least one from the Data Science group and at least one from the Theory group.

1-ENG.name: Engineering (Civil and Environmental)

select_groups.name: Core Track
select_groups.notes: Select one complete track

restricted_electives.notes: At least 4 subjects forming a coherent program under CEE faculty supervision

1-ENG.notes: 1.013 is expected to be taken twice (6 units each). 1.060 appears in multiple tracks.

2.name: Mechanical Engineering
2.notes: 2.670 is 3 units. 2.THU is 6 units minimum, up to 15 allowed. Department suggests taking a basic electronics subject early (e.g., 2.678, 6.2000, or 22.071).

2-A.name: Engineering (Mechanical Engineering)

select_groups.name: Introduction to Design Lab
select_groups.notes: 2.678 may also satisfy this if not used as required

select_groups.name: Second-Level Core
select_groups.notes: Select at least 3 second-level subjects

select_groups.name: Concentration
select_groups.notes: 6 subjects forming a coherent concentration designed with advisor. Not limited to Course 2 subjects. Must contain 150+ units of engineering content total.

2-A.notes: Flexible degree — student designs a concentration with advisor approval. Must contain at least 150 units of engineering content.

2-OE.name: Mechanical and Ocean Engineering
2-OE.degree: SB in Mechanical and Ocean Engineering
2-OE.notes: Ocean engineering focus. 2.00A is 9 units (Designing for the Future: Earth, Sea, and Space).

3.name: Materials Science and Engineering
3.degree: SB in Materials Science and Engineering

select_groups.name: Capstone
select_groups.notes: Internship (3.930+3.931) or thesis

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: 33-36 units from restricted elective list

3.notes: 3.010 and 3.020 are each 'partial CI-M' — together they count as one CI-M. 3.042 is a full CI-M.

3-A.name: Materials Science and Engineering (Flexible)

select_groups.name: Additional DMSE Core
select_groups.notes: Select at least 2 additional core subjects

select_groups.name: Proposal of Study
select_groups.notes: 66 units from a proposal of study approved by the department, forming a coherent program

3-A.notes: Flexible degree — student submits a Course 3-A Program Proposal no later than beginning of junior year.

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: Minimum 24 units from restricted elective areas

select_groups.name: Foundation Studio
select_groups.notes: Three foundation subjects from art, design, and computation

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: 48 units of restricted electives from approved list across art/design/computation/HTC

5.name: Chemistry

select_groups.name: Capstone
select_groups.notes: Option 1: remaining URIECA modules. Option 2: 5.39 Research and Communication (requires prior UROP).

5.notes: Has Standard and Flexible options. Standard requires 5.13, 5.602, 5.612. Flexible allows substituting elective focus subjects. Lab is modular (URIECA system).

5-7.name: Chemistry and Biology

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: 30 units from approved list

5-7.notes: Interdisciplinary degree spanning Chemistry and Biology departments.

7.name: Biology

select_groups.name: Intro Lab
select_groups.notes: 7.003 is the CI-M lab option

select_groups.name: Advanced Biology Electives
select_groups.notes: 48 units of advanced biology subjects

select_groups.name: Communication in Biology
select_groups.notes: At least one CI-M subject

7.notes: Requires strong chemistry foundation. 7.002 is 6 units. Need to verify exact elective count from degree chart.

8.name: Physics

select_groups.name: Math Beyond 18.03
select_groups.notes: One subject in the Department of Mathematics beyond 18.03

select_groups.name: Advanced Physics Electives
select_groups.notes: Two additional physics subjects, at least one from 8.07/8.08/8.09

8.notes: Has Focused and Flexible options. Focused is for students pursuing physics as a career. 8.THU is a 12-unit thesis. 8.13 is 18 units, 8.14 is 18 units.

9.name: Brain and Cognitive Sciences
9.degree: SB in Brain and Cognitive Sciences

select_groups.name: Core Areas
select_groups.notes: One subject from each of 4 areas: molecular/cellular neuroscience, systems neuroscience, cognitive science, and computational neuroscience

select_groups.name: Laboratory
select_groups.notes: At least one laboratory subject

select_groups.name: Electives
select_groups.notes: Additional Course 9 electives to reach required units

9.notes: Need to verify exact structure from degree chart PDF. Course 9 is distinct from 6-9 (Computation and Cognition).

10.name: Chemical Engineering

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: 21-30 units: one ChemE subject ≥6 units, one ChemE subject ≥9 units, one engineering lab ≥6 units

10.notes: ABET accredited. Students encouraged to take 5.111/5.112, 5.12, 7.01x in first year.

10-B.name: Chemical-Biological Engineering

select_groups.name: Restricted Electives
select_groups.notes: Biology/bioengineering restricted electives

10-B.notes: ABET accredited. Biological focus variant of Course 10.

10-ENG.name: Engineering (Chemical Engineering)

select_groups.name: Foundational Concepts
select_groups.notes: One from each of 3 foundational areas, selected with advisor

select_groups.name: Flexible Engineering Concentration
select_groups.notes: 4 subjects from a suggested list for the chosen concentration, or student-proposed

select_groups.name: Capstone
select_groups.notes: 12 units: senior-level project or senior thesis

10-ENG.notes: Flexible engineering degree. Student proposes concentration approved by ChemE Undergraduate Committee.

10-C.name: Chemical Engineering (Flexible)

select_groups.name: Focus Area
select_groups.notes: Coherent program of study approved by department

10-C.notes: Non-accredited flexible degree. Fewer core ChemE subjects than Course 10.

select_groups.name: Planned Electives
select_groups.notes: 78-81 units of Planning electives in consultation with advisor from Course 11 subjects

11-6.name: Urban Science and Planning with Computer Science
11-6.degree: SB in Urban Science and Planning with CS

select_groups.name: Senior Thesis/Project
select_groups.notes: Option 1: UROP + 6.UAR/11.UAR. Option 2: 11.THT + 11.THU

select_groups.name: CS Electives
select_groups.notes: Minimum 27 units of CS electives

select_groups.name: Urban Science Electives
select_groups.notes: Minimum 30 units of Urban Science electives

11-6.notes: Joint DUSP/EECS degree. 6.4590 and 11.165 can count toward Urban Planning OR Urban Science Electives, not both.
